title: Nursery Rhyme. DXXXIII. Natural History.
content:
[The following stanza is of very considerable antiquity, and is common in Yorkshire.]

        Lady-cow, lady-cow, fly thy way home,
        Thy house is on fire, thy children all gone,
        All but one that ligs under a stone,
        Fly thee home, lady-cow, ere it be gone.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. DXXXIII. Natural History."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
